[The Lagna should be in the sign in which Qulika or Gulika
navamsa is placed or its trines]
Thus Lagna longitude would be, in the trines ofGulika or
Gulika Navamsa. Thus the possibilities are-
Lagna longitude = Gulika longitude
Lagna longitude = Gulika longitude + 120 deg.
Lagna longitude = Gulika longitude + 240 deg.
Lagna longitude = Gulika longitude x 9

70 ____________________~ s~e~c~re~~~o~f~B~ir~th~~~im~e~R~e~c~tit~ic~a~ti~on
Lagna longitude = Gulika longitude x 9 + 120 deg
Lagna longitude = Gulika longitude x 9 + 240 deg
For that day or night Lagna would be (and should be) one
of these 6 signs allowed!
If all these texts are of the same opinion in emphasizing
the importance of Gulika, can we neglect this method? No,
we can't. Even then we may feel a bit reluctant to accept this
theory, since Gulika is a fixed point throughout a day or night
and forcing the presence of Lagna in the trines of Gulika or
